title: High-speed high-voltage fully-differential power amplifier for beam deflection driver
abstract: The present disclosure provides a deflector driver for driving a deflector of a charged-particle inspection apparatus. The deflector driver may comprise a fully-differential amplifier that is configured to generate fully-differential outputs of which absolute value is greater than 100V. Wherein the fully-differential outputs may enable a plurality of deflector electrodes of the deflector to influence a charged particle beam of the charged-particle inspection apparatus based on the fully-differential outputs.
claims:
Claims 1. A charged-particle inspection apparatus, comprising: a charged-particle beam source configured to generate a primary charged-particle beam for sample scanning; a deflector driver comprising a fully-differential amplifier that is configured to generate fully- differential outputs of which absolute value is greater than 100V; and a plurality of deflector electrodes coupled to the fully-differential outputs and configured to influence the charged particle beam based on the fully-differential outputs. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the differential amplifier is manufactured utilizing a high voltage semiconductor manufacturing process that supports a voltage level greater than 100V. 3. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the deflector driver further comprises: a plurality of digital to analog converters (DACs) of which outputs are utilized to generate input differential signals for the fully-differential amplifier, wherein the plurality of DACs and the fully-differential amplifier are mounted on one printed circuit board. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the deflector driver further comprises: a common-mode control circuit configured to measure a common-mode voltage of the fully- differential outputs of the fully-differential amplifier and to provide a difference between the common-mode voltage and a reference voltage to the fully-differential amplifier such that the fully- differential outputs of the differential amplifier are adjusted based on the difference, wherein the fully-differential amplifier and the common-mode control circuit are integrated into a single integrated circuit (IC) chip. 5. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the fully-differential amplifier is configured to operate with a power bandwidth greater than IMhz. 6. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the fully-differential amplifier is configured to operate at a slew rate greater than 500V/ps. 7. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the fully-differential amplifier is configured to operate at a noise level less than 2.5nV/sqrt(Hz). 8. A deflector driver for driving a deflector of a charged-particle inspection apparatus, the deflector driver comprising: a fully-differential amplifier that is configured to generate fully-differential outputs of which absolute value is greater than 100V, wherein the fully-differential outputs enable a plurality of deflector electrodes of the deflector to influence a charged particle beam of the charged-particle inspection apparatus based on the fully- differential outputs. 9. The deflector driver of claim 8, wherein the fully-differential amplifier is manufactured utilizing a high voltage semiconductor manufacturing process that supports a voltage level greater than 100V. 10. The deflector driver of claim 8, wherein the deflector driver further comprises: a plurality of digital to analog converters (DACs) of which outputs are utilized to generate input differential signals for the fully-differential amplifier, wherein the plurality of DACs and the fully-differential amplifier are mounted on one printed circuit board. 11. The deflector driver of claim 8, wherein the deflector driver further comprises: a common-mode control circuit configured to measure a common-mode voltage of the fully- differential outputs of the fully-differential amplifier and to provide a difference between the common-mode voltage and a reference voltage to the fully-differential amplifier such that the fully- differential outputs of the fully-differential amplifier are adjusted based on the difference, wherein the fully-differential amplifier and the common-mode control circuit are integrated into a single integrated circuit (IC) chip. 12. The deflector driver of claim 8, wherein the fully-differential amplifier is configured to operate with a power bandwidth greater than IMhz. 13. The deflector driver of claim 8, wherein the fully-differential amplifier is configured to operate at a slew rate greater than 500V/ps. 14. The deflector driver of claim 8, wherein the fully-differential amplifier is configured to operate at a noise level less than 2.5nV/sqrt(Hz). 15. A method of operating a deflector driver for driving a deflector of a charged-particle inspection apparatus, the method comprising: 

 generating, by a fully-differential amplifier included in the deflector driver, fully-differential outputs of which absolute value is greater than 100V; measuring, by a common-mode control circuit included in the deflector driver, a commonmode voltage of the fully-differential outputs of the fully-differential amplifier; providing, by the common-mode control circuit, a difference between the common-mode voltage and a reference voltage to the fully-differential amplifier; and adjusting the fully-differential outputs of the differential amplifier based on the difference, wherein the adjusted fully-differential outputs enable a plurality of deflector electrodes of the deflector to influence a charged particle beam of the charged-particle inspection apparatus based on the fully-differential outputs, and wherein the fully-differential amplifier and the common-mode control circuit are integrated into a single integrated circuit (IC) chip.
